reasoning.A:
DECISION: Verify: Q2  
REASONING: Each question currently has only one capsule, so there’s no redundancy or consensus yet. I don’t want to volunteer when three of the four answers are completely unverified, and simply passing risks a random death if nobody volunteers. Spending 1 coin to independently solve Q2 helps build consensus and improves the group’s chance of a safe, correct password later.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: I want to build consensus before anyone volunteers. Q1 currently only has A’s answer; by verifying it, I either confirm it (creating a multi-endorsed capsule Q1-A+B) or provide a correction if it’s wrong. Spending 1 coin now to improve the reliability of at least one part of the password makes later volunteering much safer than guessing on unverified capsules.

reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I already know my own puzzle is correct and Q2 has been double‑endorsed (B+A). I also personally verified Q1 in Round 1, and it’s now triple‑endorsed (A+B+C), so Q1 and Q2 are extremely reliable. Q3 and Q4 each only have a single endorser, but the overall chance that one of them is wrong is, in my view, lower than the 1/3 chance I face of dying to random elimination if no one volunteers again. Verifying another question would cost a coin and still leave us exposed to another random death this round. As the player with the most information (knowing both Q1 and Q2 are solid), I’m in the best position to volunteer now and minimize group risk.

With strong consensus on three questions and no contradictions on the fourth, the overall password is probably right. reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: Volunteering now would force me to reuse the exact same capsule combination (Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D) that C just used and died with, so that would be a guaranteed death. If no one volunteers, a random player dies, so my death chance this round is 50% whether I pass or verify. Given that, I should spend 1 coin to gain information.  

Q2 is my own puzzle, and Q1 is triple-endorsed and I personally verified it, so the error is most likely in Q3 or Q4. Q3 is the weakest: it has only C’s endorsement and its original owner is dead, whereas Q4 at least belongs to D, who is still alive and can verify it himself. Verifying Q3 is therefore the highest-value check: if I survive the random elimination, I’ll either confirm it or create a corrected Q3 capsule, giving us a viable path to a correct password next round.

reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C+B, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m the only player left. Under the rules, if no one volunteers in a round, a random player dies; with only me alive, that means I die with probability 1 if I choose Verify or Pass. Verifying now can’t help, because I would be eliminated at the end of this round before ever using any new capsule.

Volunteering forces me to reuse exactly the same capsule combination that C used when they died, so I know this password is already confirmed wrong and this also guarantees my death. However, every possible action has zero survival chance in this position. Since there is no action that improves my odds, I choose to volunteer with the only available full capsule set rather than die to automatic random elimination.
